matched_rules.action: scope 축소 후 재-cross-review 또는 retract

primary_decision.action: scope 축소 후 재-cross-review 또는 retract

rationale: Direct verification in openclaw tree confirms every load-bearing claim: (a) nodes.ts:312-313 does unconditional nodeWakeById.set before L333 loadApnsRegistration; (b) L334-336 no-registration branch returns without any delete; (c) the ONLY delete path is clearNodeWakeState (L525-528) wired solely from ws-connection.ts:327 under triple condition (role=node + unregister returns truthy nodeId + WS close fired); (d) PR #63709 commit 36c3a54b51 (Menglin Li, 2026-04-11 per git log — one day drift from SOL's 2026-04-09) introduced clearNodeWakeState + the single WS-close call site, but DID NOT touch the no-registration early-return branch — the gap is genuine. chosen_fix=0 (cleanup-on-early-return, 3-line body diff) is the minimal-scope primary fix: it targets the exact leak path, preserves the state.inFlight coalescing invariant (promise refs are held by awaiters directly, so deleting the map entry does not cancel in-flight work — consistent with production code at L411-415 which already nullifies state.inFlight in finally). Candidate 1 (reorder-check-before-set) is correctly rejected: the FIND root_cause_chain[0] identifies the set-before-check as intentional for concurrent coalescing, and reorder would duplicate loadApnsRegistration I/O under concurrent calls. Candidate 2 (guard-with-hasRegistration) is correctly rejected: push-apns.ts has no cheap has() API, would require an adjacent-module API addition (violates one-thing-per-PR). The __testing seam is realistic scope — the exact pattern (export const __testing) already exists at agent-wait-dedupe.ts:223 and agents.ts:78 within the same folder, so the seam addition matches established convention rather than introducing a novel observability mechanism. The seam is necessary because nodeWakeById is module-private and the leak symptom ({ lastWakeAtMs: 0 } zombies) does not affect throttle return shape — behavior-only assertions cannot observe it. The existing nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts has 4 no-registration-branch tests (L279/491/572/640) that only assert return shape, so adding delete will not break them. PR #63709 scope delineation table is concrete and accurate: the PR addressed registered-lifecycle cleanup (WS close path), this SOL extends to WS-never-completed path — distinct code paths, non-overlapping coverage. The cover-letter framing 'extends the same helper coverage to a different code path' is maintainer-receptive because it (i) explicitly references the prior PR by number + commit SHA, (ii) cites the exact gap, (iii) matches openclaw maintainer focus (memory/reliability per CLAUDE.md). change_scope=XS is realistic: ~3-line body + ~12-line __testing block + new test file, well under the 50-line XS threshold. Minor drift: SOL says #63709 merged 2026-04-09 but git shows commit date 2026-04-11 — cosmetic, doesn't affect correctness.

Attacked the 4 devil's-advocate angles and found the drafter's case holds on substance, though seam ergonomics carry residual review risk. (1) reorder-check-before-set is NOT better: nodes.ts:312-325 shows set(L313) + state.inFlight assignment(L325) + concurrent caller's `await state.inFlight` via get()(L312) share the SAME state reference — this is the coalescing invariant. Moving set after loadApnsRegistration splits the state identity between concurrent callers, causing duplicate loadApnsRegistration I/O. FIND-002 root_cause_chain[0] correctly identifies this. Candidate 0 is the minimal-delta that preserves the invariant while closing the leak. (2) __testing seam is NOT CAL-001 defense-in-depth reprise: CAL-001 was a secondary production mechanism (sweeper keep-alive) added atop an existing primary cleanup; here no primary cleanup exists for the no-registration path and the seam is purely observability with zero production behavior. Grep confirms 7+ established sibling patterns: agent-wait-dedupe.ts:223, agents.ts:78, usage.ts:355, control-plane-rate-limit.ts:112, call.ts:156, server-startup-post-attach.ts:362, openresponses-http.ts:200 — this is house style, not scope-creep. (3) PR #63709 scope-confusion close-risk: LOW. The drafter could reuse clearNodeWakeState(nodeId) helper (same API as #63709) instead of raw nodeWakeById.delete(nodeId) to explicitly frame this as 'extending the existing helper to one more call site' — this actually makes the PR narrative CLEANER and harder to close as duplicate. Recommendation: consider calling clearNodeWakeState(nodeId) in the no-registration branch instead of inlining delete, to share the helper with #63709 and simplify the scope-delineation narrative. (4) state.inFlight promise ref: already resolved in the SOL's Open Question §4 via L413-415 finally nullify — not in scope, correctly excluded. Residual risks that keep confidence at medium rather than high: (a) a strict maintainer may ask 'why add __testing when nodes.invoke-wake.test.ts already imports clearNodeWakeState directly for other tests' — the behavioral answer (lastWakeAtMs=0 entries are invisible to throttle path L321 which requires `> 0`) is airtight but requires PR-body articulation; (b) helper-reuse nuance in point 3 is a narrative refinement not called out in the current chosen_fix diff.
